title: Provider-backed live handoff isolation evidence pack

scenarios.name: http_remote_service_replay_handoff_keeps_live_lane_unblocked
scenarios.replay_path: HTTPEventLog replay through the repo-native remote event-log service while backlog delivery is intentionally gated
scenarios.live_path: events.Bus live subscriber delivery and /stream/events handoff on the runtime node
notes: The first provider-backed adapter path is the repo-native HTTP remote-service event log backed by durable SQLite storage.
A replaying subscriber is held behind the remote service gate while a live-only subscriber must still receive the next publish inside the review deadline.
After the gate opens, the replaying subscriber drains backlog and then receives the buffered live event plus the next handoff publish without duplicate replay overlap.

limitations: This proof covers the first provider-backed adapter path (`http_remote_service`), not a native broker_replicated adapter.
Live fanout still terminates through the runtime node's in-process event bus while replay and checkpoint state are served by the remote durable event-log service.
